ddls:
CREATE TABLE "health_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each record of health metrics */,
  "year" TEXT /* Year of the data record */,
  "waitlist" INTEGER /* Number of individuals on the waitlist for healthcare services */,
  "diabetes" REAL /* Percentage of the population diagnosed with diabetes */,
  "obesity" INTEGER /* Percentage of the population classified as obese */,
  "overdose" REAL /* Percentage of overdose incidents reported */,
  "pct_o" REAL /* Percentage of the population affected by a specific health metric */,
  "total_adj" REAL /* Adjusted total of healthcare-related costs or population metrics */,
  "state" TEXT /* The state from which the data was recorded */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Total population of the state for the given year */,
  "age_group" TEXT /* Age group for which the data is recorded */,
  "ethnicity" TEXT /* Ethnicity of the population for which the data is recorded */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the population for which the data is recorded */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_health_metrics_year FOREIGN KEY ("year") REFERENCES years ("year"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_health_metrics_state FOREIGN KEY ("state") REFERENCES states ("state_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "years" (
  "year_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each year */,
  "year" TEXT /* Year represented (e.g., 1995, 1996) */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the year */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the year */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("year_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "states" (
  "state_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each state */,
  "state_name" TEXT /* Name of the state */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Total population of the state */,
  "area" REAL /* Area of the state in square kilometers */,
  "capital" TEXT /* Capital city of the state */,
  "time_zone" TEXT /* Time zone(s) of the state */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("state_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., public health official, data analyst, admin) */,
  "full_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user for contact purposes */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the health metrics */,
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* ID of the health metric being accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the metric was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the device used for access */,
  "duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the access session in seconds */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_metric_id FOREIGN KEY ("metric_id") REFERENCES health_metrics ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "metrics_analysis" (
  "analysis_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each analysis result */,
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* ID of the health metric being analyzed */,
  "analysis_result" TEXT /* Results of the analysis (e.g., trends, correlations) */,
  "analysis_date" TEXT /* Date when the analysis was performed */,
  "analysis_type" TEXT /* Type of analysis performed (e.g., trend analysis, correlation analysis) */,
  "confidence_level" REAL /* Confidence level of the analysis result */,
  "methodology" TEXT /* Methodology used for the analysis */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("analysis_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_metrics_analysis_metric_id FOREIGN KEY ("metric_id") REFERENCES health_metrics ("metric_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "health_reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each health report */,
  "analysis_id" INTEGER /* ID of the analysis used to generate the report */,
  "report_content" TEXT /* Content of the health report */,
  "generated_date" TEXT /* Date when the report was generated */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* Type of report (e.g., annual, quarterly, monthly) */,
  "audience" TEXT /* Target audience for the report (e.g., public health officials, policymakers) */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the report (e.g., draft, finalized, published) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_health_reports_analysis_id FOREIGN KEY ("analysis_id") REFERENCES metrics_analysis ("analysis_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "health_interventions" (
  "intervention_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each intervention */,
  "report_id" INTEGER /* ID of the report that prompted the intervention */,
  "intervention_description" TEXT /* Description of the intervention */,
  "date_scheduled" TEXT /* Date when the intervention is scheduled to occur */,
  "intervention_type" TEXT /* Type of intervention (e.g., awareness campaign, program launch) */,
  "estimated_cost" REAL /* Estimated cost of the intervention */,
  "expected_impact" TEXT /* Expected impact of the intervention */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the intervention (e.g., planned, in progress, completed) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("intervention_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_health_interventions_report_id FOREIGN KEY ("report_id") REFERENCES health_reports ("report_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "trends" (
  "trend_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each trend */,
  "year_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the year of the trend */,
  "state_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the state related to the trend */,
  "trend_description" TEXT /* Description of the observed trend */,
  "trend_type" TEXT /* Type of trend (e.g., increasing, decreasing, stable) */,
  "trend_factor" TEXT /* Factors contributing to the trend */,
  "impact" TEXT /* Impact of the trend on public health */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("trend_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_trends_year_id FOREIGN KEY ("year_id") REFERENCES years ("year_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_trends_state_id FOREIGN KEY ("state_id") REFERENCES states ("state_id")
)
